commit.message:
[loop invariants] fix loop invariant with function calls in && chain (#3741)

## Description

Fixes #3735

Loop invariants of the form `__ESBMC_loop_invariant(A && f(x) && B)`
(function call in the middle of `&&`) were mis-handled: the generic
`remove_sideeffects` path turned the expression into short-circuit GOTO
and dropped guard `A` from the stored invariant, leading to wrong
verification results.

**Fix:**

1. **goto_sideeffects.cpp** — Special-case `__ESBMC_loop_invariant`:
flatten the top-level `&&`, run `remove_sideeffects` per conjunct,
rebuild the `and` chain. The full expression is preserved in the
LOOP_INVARIANT instruction.
2. **goto_loop_invariant.cpp** — Extract DECL/FUNCTION_CALL instructions
for invariant temporaries into a side-effects block and re-insert it
before each ASSERT/ASSUME so calls are re-evaluated after HAVOC.
3. Loops with no modified variables are no longer skipped; the base-case
ASSERT is still emitted to catch invalid invariants.

**Tests:** `github_3735` (all_cases.c: fun first/middle/last in `&&`),
`github_3735_fail` (expect FAILED), `github_3735_multi_fc` (two FCs in
`&&`).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: LukeW1999 <182648641+LukeW1999@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move useRuntimeConfig() into request interceptor (#62)

## Problem

Calling `useRuntimeConfig()` at the top level of `api/index.ts` caused
the following error on every route navigation:

```
Error: [nuxt] instance unavailable
    at useNuxtApp
    at useRuntimeConfig
```

The module is imported before any Nuxt app context exists (e.g. at
server startup), so the Nuxt instance is not yet available.

## Fix

Moved `useRuntimeConfig()` from module top-level into the axios request
interceptor. The interceptor only runs during actual HTTP requests, at
which point the Nuxt app context is always available.

commit.message:
fix: remove crossOrigin and prevent GC of in-flight Image objects

Two root causes identified for PNG assets never rendering:

1. crossOrigin='anonymous' on new Image() fails in VS Code webviews
   because the vscode-resource server may not send CORS headers.
   The retry mechanism (load without crossOrigin after CORS failure)
   could also fail due to browser response caching. Fix: remove
   crossOrigin from ALL image loads — tilesets only need drawImage()
   (works on tainted canvases), and character sheet removeBackground()
   already catches SecurityError and falls back to raw image.

2. Image objects created as local variables could be garbage collected
   before onload fires, especially under memory pressure from 7+
   simultaneous multi-MB loads. Fix: add module-level _pendingImages
   Set that holds strong references until load/error completes.

Also adds diagnostic logging: first-render asset state check in
renderer, 3-second delayed asset readiness check after character
assets message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Perf: precompute entity name counts in renderEntities (O(N) vs O(N²)) (#1071)

* Perf: precompute entity name counts in renderEntities to avoid O(N²) list scanning

Previously, for each entity in the rendered list, the code scanned the
entire entities list to count duplicates (List.filter ... List.length).
With N entities this is O(N²). Replace with a single-pass List.countBy
precomputation outside the loop, reducing to O(N).

commit.message:
feat: add update-env workflow to update .env without full setup (#31)

## Problem
Updating the `.env` file on the server required running the full
`setup-server` workflow, which also installs packages, configures
Docker, firewall, etc.

## Solution
A lightweight manual workflow that only writes the `.env` file.

## Changes

### `.github/workflows/update-env.yml` (new)
- `workflow_dispatch` only — no automatic triggers
- Same `testing` / `production` environment input as `setup-server`
- Runs the Ansible playbook with `--tags env` → only executes the "Write
.env file" task
- No bootstrap/deploy step

### `ansible/playbooks/setup-server.yml`
- Added `tags: [env]` to the "Write .env file from secrets" task so it
can be targeted independently

## Usage
GitHub → Actions → **Update Server .env** → Run workflow → choose
environment.

fix: add mobile origin to CORS_ORIGINS allowlist (#30)

## Problem
`https://mobile.${domain}` (Flutter web app) was missing from
`CORS_ORIGINS`, causing XHR network errors:
> The XMLHttpRequest onError callback was called. This typically
indicates an error on the network layer.

## Changes
- Add `https://mobile.${domain}` to `testing_cors_origins` and
`production_cors_origins` in `terraform.tfvars.example`

## Full origin list (both environments)
- `https://${domain}` — miniapp frontend
- `https://admin.${domain}` — admin frontend
- `https://mobile.${domain}` — Flutter mobile web app

commit.message:
mosquitto: add mbedTLS broker support

Rewrite mosquitto broker to use mbedTLS instead of OpenSSL for TLS,
matching the mbedTLS backend already used by the client library.

- 0002-broker-mbedtls.patch: full broker mbedTLS port
  - New src/net_mbedtls_broker.c/h: per-listener TLS context using
    mbedtls_ssl_config, srvcert, pkey, ca_chain; BIO callbacks over
    raw sockets; certificate CN extraction for logging
  - src/net.c: guard OpenSSL-specific code with WITH_TLS_OPENSSL
  - src/security_default.c: add WITH_TLS_MBEDTLS cert/key loading path
  - src/Makefile: add net_mbedtls_broker.o and tls_mbedtls.o for mbedtls
  - All other broker sources: ifdef guards for OpenSSL vs mbedTLS paths

- mosquitto-override.mk: when broker+mbedtls, exclude apps/mosquitto_ctrl
  and apps/mosquitto_passwd from build (both require OpenSSL)

Broker is opt-in via BR2_PACKAGE_THINGINO_MOSQUITTO_BROKER=y in defconfig.
